race.name: Silver Star Loppet
race.tagline: Thirty kilometers through the Okanagan highlands where Sovereign Lake's legendary snowpack fuels fast Nordic skiing.

vitals.aid_stations: 3 stations with water, electrolytes, and energy food

climate.description: The Okanagan highlands receive heavy snowfall from Pacific moisture hitting the Monashee Range. Race day temperatures typically range from -10°C to -2°C. The 1,600m altitude delivers consistent cold and deep snowpack, with conditions among the most reliable in western Canada.
challenges: Altitude at 1,600m can affect participants from lower elevations
Heavy snowfall events can coincide with race weekend
Cold temperatures combined with altitude make hydration critical
Road access to the resort can be challenging in storm conditions

course.primary: Rolling highland course through dense subalpine forest on the Sovereign Lake and Silver Star trail network. Well-designed loops with a mix of gentle climbs and flowing descents through snow-laden spruce and fir forest.
course.grooming: Excellent grooming on one of BC's most extensive Nordic trail networks (105km total)
features: Part of a 105km groomed trail network — one of the largest in Canada
Deep reliable snowpack from Okanagan Highland precipitation
Adjacent to Silver Star ski resort village for post-race amenities

nordic_lab_rating.score_note: Sum 39/70 = 55.71, rounds to 56. Outstanding snow reliability and trail network quality elevate an otherwise modest regional event.
nordic_lab_rating.tier_note: Score 56 >= 45 but < 60, TIER 3. No prestige override (prestige=2).

history.summary: Established in 2001 on the Sovereign Lake Nordic Centre trails near Silver Star Mountain Resort. The area has become one of British Columbia's premier Nordic skiing destinations thanks to its exceptional snowpack and extensive trail network. The loppet draws participants from across BC and Alberta, serving as a key event in the Okanagan Nordic racing calendar.
notable_facts: Sovereign Lake Nordic Centre has over 105km of groomed trails
The area receives an average of 7 meters of snowfall annually
Silver Star village is one of BC's few ski-in, ski-out resort communities
The trail network connects to the Sovereign to Silver Star point-to-point race route

youtube_data.search_query: Silver Star Loppet Vernon cross country ski race Sovereign Lake
